tagline: Now generally available for text classification.
what_it_does: The `ai_classify` function enables users to classify text content into predefined categories based on custom labels, supporting features like multi-label classification and label descriptions. It is designed to work with unstructured data stored in Unity Catalog, allowing for efficient document classification. Users can create classification agents through a no-code UI to streamline the classification process and improve accuracy over time.
why_it_matters: This release enhances productivity by providing a robust tool for automating text classification, which can lead to improved decision-making and operational efficiency. Businesses can leverage this capability to better understand customer interactions, categorize documents, and analyze sentiment, ultimately driving better customer experiences and insights.
plain_summary: The ai classify function enables users to classify text content into predefined categories based on custom labels, supporting features like multi label classification and label descriptions. It is designed to work with unstructured data stored in Unity…

capabilities.title: Multi-Label Classification
capabilities.desc: Classify documents with multiple labels to capture complex categorization needs.

capabilities.title: Custom Label Descriptions
capabilities.desc: Define and refine classification labels with optional descriptions for clarity.

capabilities.title: No-Code UI for Agents
capabilities.desc: Utilize a user-friendly interface to create and manage classification agents without coding.

capabilities.title: Integration with SQL and Spark
capabilities.desc: Run classification at scale using SQL queries or create ETL pipelines for automated processing.

prerequisites: Serverless compute enabled in the workspace.
Access to a serverless usage policy with a nonzero budget.
Unstructured data must be in a Unity Catalog volume or table.
At least one unlabeled document or row in the data source.
use_cases: Classifying customer call transcripts by intent.
Organizing unstructured data for better retrieval.
limitations: Function availability is limited to specific regions.
Requires compliance with regional support for Enhanced Security and Compliance add-on.

steps.title: Select Source Data
steps.desc: Choose a volume or table containing the documents to classify.

steps.title: Configure Labels
steps.desc: Add and refine classification labels manually or import from a table.

steps.title: Review Classification Responses
steps.desc: Provide feedback on classification accuracy to improve the agent.

steps.title: Use the Classification Agent
steps.desc: Deploy the agent for large-scale classification using SQL or Spark pipelines.

steps.title: Iterate and Optimize
steps.desc: Compare versions of the agent to enhance performance based on feedback.

key_takeaway: The `ai_classify` function empowers organizations to automate and enhance text classification, driving better insights and operational efficiency.
